title: NotEnoughGlints
description: A resourcepack allowing non-modded players to color the glint of their enchanted items
body:
This resource + data pack enables custom enchantment glint options, with support for all vanilla dyes, you gain the ability to personalize your tools and armor to your liking!

![Display of custom glinted armor & tools](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/RRCbhkMA/images/a8aeabbd8d9f2a60a7ef80ec4bc6110cbd1a21bf.gif)

This pack modifies the item and entity shaders, and won't work in cojunction with others who do the same.

The Resourcepack can be used without the datapack, but you'll lack the recipes, so be aware you'll only be able to summon custom glinted items via commands, resources on that are coming soon.

Does work on 26.1.2+, However requires OpenGL to be preffered.

to custom-glint a piece of gear, place it + a dye + a honeycomb together in a smithing table.
removing glints doesnt work yet, however you can change between them freely.
use a nether star for the rainbow glint effect.

![Showcasing the Recipe for a custom enchantment glint, a honeycomb + the piece of gear to be Glinted + dye of a certain color or nether star](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/RRCbhkMA/images/916d1a331ede238b75cd9c2e50999730010db783.gif)

due to a technical limitation with the current shader implementation, its not available on leather armor. ):

Upcoming features:
- fallback textures to vanilla textures, so when removing the Resourcepack items with colored glint wont appear as missing texture.
- implementing needed things to allow Colored Glints shields
- making a version for people who use the "Glint Speed" accessibility option

youre welcome to join the discord for Sneak peeks into the development progress regarding future features, or just to ask questions regarding the pack
